_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to analyse data distributions from different sampling techniques.

_source.w: We are learning to analyse data distributions from different sampling techniques.
y: Different sampling methods can produce different results — understanding this helps us evaluate the reliability of data.
Analysing distributions from various samples develops our ability to draw valid conclusions from imperfect data.
These analytical skills are used in medical research, market research, environmental monitoring and education.
eg: Comparing distributions from random samples, convenience samples and stratified samples of the same population.
Analysing how sample size affects the shape and spread of the distribution.
Identifying skew, outliers and clustering in sample distributions.
Discussing why a convenience sample (e.g., surveying only friends) may not represent the wider population.
Comparing histograms from two different sampling methods and evaluating which is more representative.
Using technology to generate multiple random samples and comparing their distributions.
